steps.title: Identify what emphasizes harshness
steps.content: The goal requires emphasizing harshness concretely. The strongest signal is the winter temperature of −30°F in the notes—a specific, extreme detail that creates vivid emphasis, unlike general statements about adaptation or plant counts.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly uses the −30°F detail paired with descriptive language (especially harsh, frigid), combining specific evidence with direct emphasis to make conditions sound genuinely extreme.

The other choices omit harsh details, introduce the contradictory mild temperature, or focus on adaptation and biodiversity rather than harshness.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
